title: Gap record naming does not follow the workstream convention: files are <source>-G<n>.json (e.g. lume-G2.json) with bare sequential ids, whereas workstreams use <id>-<stub> (e.g. 0017-review-dir-and-landing) so listings sort sequentially and the name carries a human-readable hint
context: Operator direction 2026-06-11: align gap naming with the workstream convention, i.e. <id>-<stub> (id zero-padded for ordering, stub a slugified title hint). Touches gap.py id minting/filenames, the adopter scan's dedupe key parsing, lume gap resolve addressing, docs, and a migration of the existing .lume/gaps/ records.

resolution.note: Implemented by 0019: filenames are <source>-G<nnnn>-<stub>.json (padded + slugified in the name only, (source,id) identity unchanged); 9 records git-mv'd; legacy names still read and migrate on write
